instruction: In ship2, bootstrap a runnable React + Vite + TypeScript app in place. Install dependencies. Build a real UI with a left document sidebar, a top bar, and a main editor placeholder so there is something visual to test. Keep it minimal but polished. Finish only when package.json, src/, and working dev/build scripts exist in /Users/maxpetrusenko/Desktop/Gauntlet/ship2.

steps.description: Bootstrap the Vite React TypeScript app manifest and entry scaffolding in place so the project becomes installable and runnable. Create the missing package.json with dev/build/preview scripts and React/Vite/TypeScript dependencies, add the root HTML entry, add TypeScript config files compatible with Vite, and replace the stray JS-only Vite config with a proper TypeScript config.

Create the React application source tree under src/ with the Vite entry point and a minimal but polished UI shell: top bar, left document sidebar, and main editor placeholder. Include component styling and global styles so the app is visually testable immediately.
Update the minimal project documentation to reflect the new bootstrap state and how to run the installed app locally, keeping it aligned with the actual scripts and app structure created in place.

    "target": "ES2022",

  "include": ["src"]
}
